summary: Payroll Rounding Fix Verification - All payroll-related tests pass. The fix ensures payroll reports use individually rounded shift hours (sum of rounded hours, not rounded sum). Backend API returns rounded_hours and estimated_pay fields in shift report summary. Frontend uses these fields for consistent display.

minor.issue: 4 W9 tests failing due to hardcoded document IDs (a0de9d4c-58d6-4c9c-9add-b66000604bc1) that no longer exist. Not related to payroll rounding changes.

details.payroll-rounding.spec.ts: 9/9 passed - New payroll rounding fix tests
details.reports-est-pay.spec.ts: 9/9 passed - Existing Est. Pay column tests
details.hours-format-clockin.spec.ts: 10/10 passed - Hours format and clock-in tests
details.pytest: 45/49 passed - 4 W9 tests failing due to hardcoded IDs (unrelated to changes)

seed_data_creation: Used existing time entries for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Payroll rounding fix verified and working. The key change is that shift reports now include rounded_hours and estimated_pay fields in the summary, which the frontend uses directly. This ensures consistency: sum(individually rounded shift hours) = displayed total hours. W9 API tests have hardcoded document IDs that need updating but are unrelated to payroll changes.
rca_of_issue: The original payroll discrepancy was caused by two different rounding approaches: (1) summing raw hours then rounding, vs (2) rounding each shift individually then summing. Approach #2 is correct for user expectations since it matches what they see per-shift. The fix updated the backend to calculate rounded_hours and estimated_pay using approach #2, and the frontend now uses these pre-calculated values.
